name: Tetum (Tetun Praça)

ancestors.notes: Indonesian adstrate: Indonesian was imposed as an administrative language during occupation (1975–1999); contributed loanwords especially in formal/technical registers.

notes: Tetum (Tetun Praça / Tetun Dili). Austronesian language (Malayo-Polynesian); co-official language of East Timor alongside Portuguese. ~800,000 speakers including L2. Tetun Praça (urban Dili creolized variety) is the official variety, heavily influenced by Portuguese. KEY FEATURES: (1) FINAL OBSTRUENTS: final -k may realize as [ʔ] (glottal stop) — Austronesian feature. (2) PALATAL CONSONANTS: lh=[ʎ], nh=[ɲ], x=[ʃ], j=[dʒ] — borrowed graphemic conventions from Portuguese. (3) NASAL VELAR: ng=[ŋ] may appear word-initially. (4) VOWEL ALTERNATION: e varies [e]~[ɛ], o varies [o]~[ɔ] depending on stress and register. (5) DIPHTHONGS: ai=[aj], au=[aw]. (6) V: phoneme /v/ from Portuguese loanwords; absent in indigenous Austronesian vocabulary. (7) TETUN TERIK: rural traditional variety (Tetum Terik / Tetun Belu) has more conservative phonology and less Portuguese influence — this file models Tetun Praça in the INL standardised orthography (Government Decree 1/2004).

sources.author: Van Klinken, C.
sources.title: A Grammar of the Fehan Dialect of Tetun
sources.notes: Descriptive grammar; includes phonological comparison with Tetun Praça

sources.author: Williams-van Klinken, C., Hajek, J. & Nordlinger, R.
sources.title: Tetun Dili: A Grammar of an East Timorese Language
sources.notes: Primary descriptive grammar of Tetun Praça/Dili

stress.notes: Stress falls on the penultimate syllable in the large majority of words (Williams-van Klinken, Hajek & Nordlinger 2002, Tetun Dili: A Grammar of an East Timorese Language, Pacific Linguistics, ch. 2). Native diphthongs ai [aj] and au [aw] are declared so the syllabifier counts each as a single nucleus; the Portuguese-loan sequences ei and ou are usually monophthongised in ordinary speech and are treated as hiatus.

orthography_standard.name: Matadalan Ortográfiku ba Tetun Ofisiál (INL standard orthography)
orthography_standard.authority: Instituto Nacional de Linguística (INL), Universidade Nacional Timor Lorosa'e
orthography_standard.notes: Declared the official orthography of Tetun by Government Decree 1/2004 (14 April 2004); the INL is the scientific custodian of Official Tetun. This spec targets that standard: the apostrophe for the glottal stop, the digraphs lh/nh/ng and the letters x/j taken over from Portuguese conventions.
